backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor defining the FocusRoom backend with room management, user profiles, session control, and authorization via AccessControl mixin. Implements CRUD for rooms, participants, sessions, and events using Map/Set data structures.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting a single feature requiring a full rewrite of CameraPreview.tsx camera toggle logic to call getUserMedia directly, apply permanent blur, handle errors, and clean up on unmount.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Text file containing JSON summaries of all frontend source files, describing the purpose and key behaviors of each component, page, hook, and CSS file in the project.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS entry point defining CSS custom property tokens for light and dark themes (colors, borders, chart colors, sidebar) used throughout the app.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell for the FocusRoom SPA, mounting the React app at #root and loading the main TypeScript entry point.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Router with routes for login, lobby, room, and index (redirects to lobby), wrapped in a forced-dark ThemeProvider and Toaster.
frontend/src/components/auth/ProfileSetu: Blocking modal dialog that forces new users to set a username (minimum 2 characters) before accessing the app, using the saveCallerUserProfile mutation.
frontend/src/components/layout/AppLayout: Persistent app shell with a sticky header (logo, auth controls, username display), footer with attribution, and conditional rendering of ProfileSetupModal for users without a profile.
frontend/src/components/room/AmbientSoun: A dropdown toggle component that lets users select and play looping ambient sounds (rain, library, white noise) during focus phases, persisting the selection to localStorage.
frontend/src/components/room/CameraPrevi: A camera preview component that requests webcam access via getUserMedia, handles various permission/device error states, and renders the live video feed with start/stop controls.
frontend/src/components/room/ChatPanel.t: A chat panel component that displays and sends messages within a room, blurring all messages during focus phases and storing messages as backend events.
frontend/src/components/room/Distraction: A focus-phase indicator that detects when the user leaves the browser tab for more than 10 seconds and displays a 'Stay focused!' warning overlay.
frontend/src/components/room/GoalComplet: A modal dialog shown at session end that asks the user whether they completed their stated goal, with XP reward information and yes/no actions.
frontend/src/components/room/GoalDisplay: A simple display component that renders the user's current focus goal with a target icon inside a styled card.
frontend/src/components/room/GoalEntryMo: A modal dialog that prompts the user to enter a focus goal before a session starts, with a textarea, submit, and skip option.
frontend/src/components/room/Leaderboard: A leaderboard panel that sorts and displays session XP for all participants, highlighting the current user and showing medal icons for the top three.
frontend/src/components/room/Participant: A participant list component that displays room members; currently only shows the current user locally since the backend lacks a getParticipants endpoint.
frontend/src/components/room/TimerDispla: Renders a Pomodoro-style timer with an SVG progress ring, phase label (Focus/Break/Idle), countdown digits, and a start button, including a glow animation when the focus phase begins.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, and invalidates all dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useGetCallerUserProfi: Custom hook that fetches the current authenticated user's profile from the backend actor using React Query, waiting until the actor is ready before querying.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and provider, exposing login/logout functions, identity state, and login status via a React context and custom hook.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for room creation/joining, timer state polling, session starting, and storing chat/goal events against the ICP backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useSaveCallerUserProf: Custom hook that exposes a mutation to save the caller's user profile to the backend and invalidates the cached profile query on success.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports fonts, defines Tailwind CSS layers with dark-mode OKLCH design tokens, and declares custom component styles and keyframe animations (timer glow, distraction pulse, camera blur).
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with React Query and Internet Identity providers wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/LobbyPage.tsx: Lobby page that lets authenticated users create or join a focus room by code, with automatic rejoin of the last visited room stored in localStorage.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Login page component that displays the FocusRoom branding, feature list, and an Internet Identity login button, redirecting to /lobby once authenticated.
frontend/src/pages/RoomPage.tsx: Main study room page that manages Pomodoro timer state (derived from server session), local countdown, goal entry/completion flow, XP tracking, and renders all room sub-components like chat, camera, leaderboard, and participant list.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility module providing functions to read URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routes, persist them in sessionStorage, and clean sensitive params from the URL bar.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom fonts, OKLCH-based color tokens for the dark FocusRoom theme (room-bg, focus-accent, break-accent, etc.), border radii, shadows, and animation keyframes.
project_state.json: Project state manifest (version 11) describing the FocusRoom platform's architecture, existing features, known issues (broken room creation, camera not working), and overall product summary.
